Exact_Claim: Domestic banks and bondholders were the principal mechanism preventing immediate collapse.
People_and_Entities: Donald Trump; Citibank; Bankers Trust; Chase; creditor committee
Evidence_Status: Established by SEC bankruptcy and documentary records
Supporting_Evidence: Coordinated creditor workout; restructurings; equity concessions; asset sales; limited new credit.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Exact allocation of each bank's exposure and concessions remains incomplete.
Recommended_Wording: Domestic creditors and bondholders supplied the decisive immediate survival mechanism.
Open_Question: Which lenders bore the largest economic losses and what side agreements preserved Trump control?
Additional_Record_Needed: Workout agreements; bank committee minutes; loan schedules; personal guarantee releases.

Exact_Claim: Trump Plaza principal was reduced and maturity extended in the 1992 restructuring.
People_and_Entities: Trump Plaza Associates; Plaza Funding; bondholders
Supporting_Evidence: SEC records principal reduction from $250m to $225m; rate reduction; maturity extension; stock units.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: SEC filing summarizes rather than reproduces the full plan.
Recommended_Wording: The 1992 Plaza restructuring reduced principal, extended maturity, lowered interest, and gave bondholders equity-linked value.
Open_Question: What rights and controls did the stock units and managing partner structure confer?
Additional_Record_Needed: Chapter 11 plan; disclosure statement; partnership agreement; bond indenture.

Exact_Claim: Taj Mahal failed to file required casino currency reports during its opening period.
People_and_Entities: Trump Taj Mahal Associates; IRS; FinCEN
Supporting_Evidence: FinCEN violation period April 1990 through December 1991; $477,000 settlement.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: FinCEN also described otherwise laudable compliance and cooperation; customers unidentified.
Recommended_Wording: Opening-period CTR failures created a documented cash-reporting vulnerability.
Open_Question: Which customers and transactions generated the missed reports?
Additional_Record_Needed: IRS examination workpapers; FinCEN case file; CTR exception logs; casino cage records.

Exact_Claim: Hong Kong-led investors rescued Riverside South by addressing debt and financing development.
People_and_Entities: Henry Cheng; Vincent Lo; Polylinks; Chase; Trump entities
Evidence_Status: Well-supported contemporaneous reporting; transaction records needed
Supporting_Evidence: Contemporaneous UPI and later transaction reporting describe financing, debt assumption, and retained Trump minority role.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Exact capital contributions and profit waterfall require agreements.
Recommended_Wording: Asian investors removed a major development burden while leaving Trump a minority profit and management position.
Open_Question: Were there side letters, guaranteed fees, or reciprocal investments?
Additional_Record_Needed: Partnership agreement; debt purchase; management agreement; capital ledger; beneficial ownership.

Exact_Claim: Saudi and Singaporean investors acquired control of the Plaza as part of a creditor-driven exit.
People_and_Entities: Prince Alwaleed; CDL/Kwek; Citibank group; Donald Trump
Supporting_Evidence: American Banker and contemporaneous reports identify $325m valuation, mortgage support, renovation obligation, diminished Trump role.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Precise retained percentage and later rights vary among reports.
Recommended_Wording: The Plaza transaction removed liability and preserved only limited Trump participation.
Open_Question: What management, condominium-development, or future-deal rights survived?
Additional_Record_Needed: Purchase agreement; mortgage modification; management agreement; partnership records.

Exact_Claim: THCR raised $140m equity and $155m secured debt in 1995.
People_and_Entities: THCR; public shareholders; bond investors; Donald Trump
Supporting_Evidence: SEC filing states 10m shares at $14 and $155m 15.5% notes.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Use-of-proceeds allocations require prospectus detail.
Recommended_Wording: Public investors became a major source of casino recovery capital in 1995.
Open_Question: How much value flowed to Trump personally or to privately controlled entities?
Additional_Record_Needed: Prospectus; underwriting agreement; contribution agreement; funds-flow memorandum.

Exact_Claim: The 1996 Taj transaction raised $386.1m equity and $1.2b mortgage debt.
People_and_Entities: THCR; Trump AC; public investors; underwriters
Supporting_Evidence: SEC 10-K records 13.25m shares and $1.2b notes.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Does not establish that the capital structure was prudent or fraudulent.
Recommended_Wording: The Taj acquisition was financed overwhelmingly through public equity and high-yield mortgage debt.
Open_Question: Which obligations, guarantees, and private interests were released or paid?
Additional_Record_Needed: Prospectus; closing statement; lien-release schedule; underwriter files.

Exact_Claim: Trump received interests convertible into 5,837,700 THCR shares for Castle interests valued at $168.126m.
People_and_Entities: Donald Trump; TCI-II; THCR; Castle Associates
Supporting_Evidence: SEC accounting and transaction description.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Valuation was contested; final litigation outcome not yet located.
Recommended_Wording: Trump transferred Castle interests into THCR and received publicly convertible value while increasing his beneficial position.
Open_Question: Was the appraisal fair and independent?
Additional_Record_Needed: Fairness opinion; appraisal; board minutes; special committee files; final court disposition.

Exact_Claim: Shareholders alleged excessive price and self-dealing in the Castle acquisition.
People_and_Entities: THCR shareholders; directors; Trump; Salomon Brothers
Evidence_Status: Civil allegation not adjudicated in located record
Supporting_Evidence: SEC filing describes consolidated derivative complaints and specific allegations.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Company denied claims; disposition required before stating liability.
Recommended_Wording: Shareholder suits alleged excessive self-dealing and misleading proxy disclosures; the allegation must not be presented as a final finding.
Open_Question: How were the cases resolved?
Additional_Record_Needed: SDNY docket 96 Civ. 7820; Delaware dockets 15148/15160; settlement or dismissal orders.

Exact_Claim: The public-company structure shifted substantial downside to shareholders and bondholders while preserving Trump control.
People_and_Entities: THCR; shareholders; bondholders; Donald Trump
Evidence_Status: Analytical inference strongly supported by SEC records
Supporting_Evidence: Public offerings, retained voting interests, related-party transfer, stock decline, recurring debt.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: External capital also legitimately financed operations; not proof of unlawful extraction.
Recommended_Wording: THCR externalized substantial capital risk while preserving Trump’s brand, voting power, and economic interest.
Open_Question: What were investor returns and Trump compensation over the same period?
Additional_Record_Needed: Annual reports; proxy statements; stock and bond total-return series; compensation records.

Exact_Claim: THCR stock fell from a 1996 high above $35 to below $9 by March 1997.
People_and_Entities: THCR public shareholders
Supporting_Evidence: SEC 10-K quarterly high/low table.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Market price alone does not prove wrongdoing.
Recommended_Wording: Public shareholders experienced a rapid loss of market value after the expansion transactions.
Open_Question: What disclosures or events drove the decline?
Additional_Record_Needed: Contemporaneous analyst reports; earnings calls; press releases; trading-event study.

Exact_Claim: Trump’s 1996 Moscow trip was facilitated by a U.S. business network with existing Russian access.
People_and_Entities: Donald Trump; Howard Lorber; Bennett LeBow; David Geovanis; Liggett-Ducat/Vector
Evidence_Status: Established trip and network; full project records missing
Supporting_Evidence: Contemporaneous and Senate-linked reporting identifies participants and Ducat project access.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No completed Trump project or financing identified.
Recommended_Wording: The 1996 trip joined Trump’s recovering brand to American intermediaries already embedded in Russian commerce.
Open_Question: What terms, financing, sites, and Russian sponsors were discussed?
Additional_Record_Needed: Travel records; project proposals; letters; visa sponsor; meeting notes; financing term sheets.

Exact_Claim: The Senate investigated allegations that Geovanis knew of possible compromising material.
People_and_Entities: David Geovanis; SSCI; Donald Trump
Supporting_Evidence: Senate Volume 5 preserves the investigative issue.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Public evidence does not establish existence or possession of kompromat; reliability concerns exist.
Recommended_Wording: Geovanis was a relevant access intermediary and the subject of an unproven Senate-investigated kompromat lead.
Open_Question: What testimony and exhibits remain redacted?
Additional_Record_Needed: SSCI interview transcript; exhibits; FBI referrals; travel and event records.

Exact_Claim: ULLICO financed 40 Wall Street with a union-labor contractor condition.
People_and_Entities: ULLICO; Trump entities; Deutsche Bank affiliate; contractors
Evidence_Status: Recorded-document reporting plus ULLICO program description
Supporting_Evidence: Mortgage-record reporting identifies $10m and $8m loans, union rider, assignment and additional Deutsche financing.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Original loan instruments should be retrieved; condition itself is lawful.
Recommended_Wording: Institutional financing could require use of a defined labor-contractor network.
Open_Question: Did contractors overlap with Chapter 2 cartel-linked suppliers or connected investors?
Additional_Record_Needed: ACRIS mortgages and riders; contractor list; procurement files; mechanics liens.

Exact_Claim: Trump contributed only a small portion of equity in the GM Building acquisition.
People_and_Entities: Donald Trump; Conseco; Lehman Brothers
Supporting_Evidence: Conseco reportedly supplied $211m, Trump $11m, with roughly $700m mortgage.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Exact original closing statement not in repository.
Recommended_Wording: The GM Building exemplified a brand-and-management model funded mostly by a partner and leverage.
Open_Question: Were there side guarantees or contingent profit rights?
Additional_Record_Needed: Purchase agreement; partnership agreement; mortgage; arbitration record.

Exact_Claim: Trump World Tower received approximately $295m in institutional construction financing.
People_and_Entities: 845 UN LP; ULLICO; HypoVereinsbank; Deutsche Bank
Supporting_Evidence: Mortgage reporting identifies lenders, amount, labor rider, later assignment.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Complete capital stack and tranche amounts not yet reconstructed.
Recommended_Wording: Trump World Tower relied on large institutional construction loans and later loan assignments.
Open_Question: How much sponsor equity and presale value was required?
Additional_Record_Needed: ACRIS mortgage package; building loan agreement; draw schedules; guarantees; assignments.

Exact_Claim: Trump World Tower began presales before construction.
People_and_Entities: 845 UN LP; Donald Trump; purchasers
Evidence_Status: Established by New York appellate decision
Supporting_Evidence: Uzan decision states sales began October 1998 and building was unconstructed in April 1999.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Does not identify all purchasers or lender requirements.
Recommended_Wording: The project used binding preconstruction sales as part of its development model.
Open_Question: What percentage of units/value was presold before each construction draw?
Additional_Record_Needed: Offering plan; contract ledger; lender certifications; sales reports.

Exact_Claim: Uzan-related buyers signed seven purchase agreements before construction.
People_and_Entities: Cem Uzan; Hakan Uzan; Antonio Betancourt; 845 UN LP
Supporting_Evidence: Court details seven agreements, four penthouses, three later closings.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No evidence Trump knew of later Motorola fraud at contract date.
Recommended_Wording: A wealthy foreign group committed to multiple units before construction and received negotiated bulk terms.
Open_Question: Who introduced the buyers and conducted due diligence?
Additional_Record_Needed: Purchaser files; broker records; KYC/source-of-funds; correspondence.

Exact_Claim: Four Uzan penthouses received a discount exceeding $7m to about $32m total.
People_and_Entities: Uzan buyers; 845 UN LP
Supporting_Evidence: Court records negotiated price and discount.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: A negotiated discount is not inherently improper.
Recommended_Wording: The sponsor granted a major bulk-purchase discount to sophisticated foreign buyers.
Open_Question: Were similar terms offered to other bulk or connected buyers?
Additional_Record_Needed: Offering-plan price schedule; concession ledger; all amended contracts.

Exact_Claim: Uzan buyers obtained unusual assignment and resale rights.
People_and_Entities: Uzan buyers; 845 UN LP
Supporting_Evidence: Court says assignment right was not granted to any other purchaser according to sponsor counsel.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: May reflect bargaining power rather than reciprocity.
Recommended_Wording: The bulk purchasers received nonstandard pre-closing transfer rights.
Open_Question: Did those rights help market or finance the project?
Additional_Record_Needed: Contract drafts; sponsor counsel files; lender review; resale efforts.

Exact_Claim: Approximately $8m in disputed penthouse deposits was held in escrow.
People_and_Entities: Uzan buyers; escrow agent; 845 UN LP
Supporting_Evidence: Court specifies staged 25% down payments and escrow.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Escrow limits direct-use claim; release terms not known.
Recommended_Wording: Large presale deposits existed, but direct construction use is not established.
Open_Question: Were deposits released or counted toward lender thresholds?
Additional_Record_Needed: Escrow agreement; bank statements; offering amendments; lender covenants; architect certifications.

Exact_Claim: Bulk presales may have helped unlock construction finance.
People_and_Entities: 845 UN LP; lenders; bulk buyers
Supporting_Evidence: Presales existed before completion; construction lending was substantial; such tests are common.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No located covenant yet ties Uzan contracts to advances; funds were escrowed.
Recommended_Wording: Presales could have reduced lender risk or satisfied sales tests, but the link must be documented.
Open_Question: What exact presale threshold did lenders require?
Additional_Record_Needed: Building loan agreement; borrowing-base tests; draw certificates; presale schedules.

Exact_Claim: Capital provider required union contractors at Trump projects.
People_and_Entities: ULLICO; contractors; Trump entities
Supporting_Evidence: Recorded-document reporting and ULLICO program require union construction.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Lawful labor requirement is not a mob or kickback finding.
Recommended_Wording: The financing documents demonstrate that capital could be conditioned on a specific contractor class.
Open_Question: Which firms received work, and did they overlap with connected buyers or older cartel networks?
Additional_Record_Needed: Original rider; contractor/subcontractor list; union signatory records; payment applications.

Exact_Claim: Kislin and Sapir’s Joy-Lud supplied televisions to an early Trump hotel.
People_and_Entities: Sam Kislin; Tamir Sapir; Joy-Lud; Trump hotel entities
Supporting_Evidence: Repeated documentary accounts; continuity with later roles.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Underlying invoice and credit agreement not yet obtained.
Recommended_Wording: An early supplier relationship linked Trump to businessmen serving Soviet diplomatic customers.
Open_Question: Was credit extended, and who negotiated the order?
Additional_Record_Needed: Invoices; purchase orders; credit terms; hotel procurement records.

Exact_Claim: A 1994 FBI report allegedly listed Kislin as an Ivankov member/associate.
People_and_Entities: Sam Kislin; FBI; Ivankov
Supporting_Evidence: Center for Public Integrity reports confidential FBI document.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Kislin denied knowing Ivankov and was not charged.
Recommended_Wording: An FBI intelligence report reportedly associated Kislin with Ivankov; the allegation remains unadjudicated.
Open_Question: Can the underlying report be obtained or authenticated?
Additional_Record_Needed: FBI FOIA; Ivankov file; source reliability; declassification request.

Exact_Claim: Interpol alleged Trans Commodities was used in fraud and laundering involving Chernoy interests.
People_and_Entities: Trans Commodities; Sam Kislin; Mikhail and Lev Chernoy; Interpol
Supporting_Evidence: CPI quotes 1996 Interpol report and Kislin admission that Mikhail worked for him.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Kislin denied laundering; USAO declined civil action.
Recommended_Wording: Interpol and FBI allegations placed Kislin’s firm inside a suspect post-Soviet commodities network, but no criminal case was brought against him.
Open_Question: What transactions and accounts were examined?
Additional_Record_Needed: Interpol report; Russian MLAT request; bank records; USAO declination letter.

Exact_Claim: Kislin later issued private mortgages to Trump World Tower purchasers.
People_and_Entities: Sam Kislin; Trans Commodities; Vasily Salygin; TWT buyers
Supporting_Evidence: Bloomberg-derived reporting identifies specific mortgage relationship.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Most closings postdate Chapter 3 and full mortgage series not yet compiled.
Recommended_Wording: The early Trump supplier later became a private-credit intermediary for post-Soviet Trump-property buyers.
Open_Question: How many loans, to whom, and from what source of funds?
Additional_Record_Needed: ACRIS mortgage/deed/assignment/satisfaction ledger; lender bank records.

Exact_Claim: Sapir used Soviet diplomatic customers and commodity trades to build real-estate capital.
People_and_Entities: Tamir Sapir; Joy-Lud; Soviet officials; Russian oil entities
Supporting_Evidence: Forbes describes Soviet diplomatic customer base and exchange of electronics for fertilizer/oil rights.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Not proof of intelligence role or illegal trade.
Recommended_Wording: Sapir converted Soviet commercial access into commodity wealth and then Manhattan real estate.
Open_Question: Which Soviet entities and officials were counterparties?
Additional_Record_Needed: Joy-Lud contracts; export licenses; Russian arbitration/court records; bank transfers.

Exact_Claim: Sapir’s early supplier relationship later developed into Trump development partnership.
People_and_Entities: Tamir Sapir; Donald Trump; Sapir Organization
Evidence_Status: Network continuity established; later transaction belongs Chapter 4
Supporting_Evidence: Early hotel supply and later Trump SoHo partnership are documented.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No evidence early transaction was arranged to create later influence.
Recommended_Wording: Sapir is a long-duration bridge from Soviet-facing commerce into later Trump development capital.
Open_Question: What interactions occurred between the early order and Trump SoHo?
Additional_Record_Needed: Correspondence; tenancy; deals; social events; financing proposals.

Exact_Claim: Ivankov was a powerful Russian crime figure prosecuted in 1995.
People_and_Entities: Vyacheslav Ivankov; DOJ; FBI
Supporting_Evidence: DOJ annual report describes indictment and reputed status.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Official source does not itself prove Trump-property residence.
Recommended_Wording: Ivankov’s prosecution is established; reported use of Trump properties requires underlying exhibits.
Open_Question: Which Trump units, hotel rooms, or contact numbers appear in FBI/court records?
Additional_Record_Needed: Indictment; trial exhibits; search inventories; surveillance reports; property records.

Exact_Claim: Ivankov reportedly lived or operated in Trump-associated properties.
People_and_Entities: Ivankov; Trump Tower; Taj Mahal; Trump Organization
Evidence_Status: Strong investigative reporting; primary corroboration incomplete
Supporting_Evidence: Multiple investigative accounts cite FBI investigators and phonebook evidence.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Presence does not prove Trump knowledge; underlying records not yet obtained.
Recommended_Wording: Investigative reporting places Ivankov inside the Trump property network, subject to primary-record confirmation.
Open_Question: What ownership, guest, surveillance, and telephone records exist?
Additional_Record_Needed: FBI FOIA; court exhibits; condominium deeds; casino hotel logs.

Exact_Claim: Approximately 65 Trump World Tower units were sold to Russian buyers.
People_and_Entities: Dolly Lenz; Russian buyers; TWT
Evidence_Status: Broker statement; transaction ledger not completed
Supporting_Evidence: Lenz’s statement repeatedly reported.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Chronology likely spans early 2000s; not 65 completed late-1990s closings.
Recommended_Wording: A substantial Russian buyer constituency developed from the presale period into the early 2000s.
Open_Question: Which 65 units and beneficial owners?
Additional_Record_Needed: Sponsor closing ledger; deeds; passports/addresses; broker commission files.

Exact_Claim: Russian money was not the principal immediate bailout but became important to the rebuilding model.
People_and_Entities: Trump entities; creditors; foreign investors; post-Soviet buyers
Supporting_Evidence: Bank rescue and non-Russian foreign deals precede Russian condo expansion; later demand and private credit documented.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Unrecorded Russian capital could exist; precise totals unavailable.
Recommended_Wording: Domestic creditors and non-Russian foreign investors prevented collapse; post-Soviet capital increasingly supplied demand, credit, and access during rebuilding.
Open_Question: What is the annual dollar amount of verified post-Soviet inflows?
Additional_Record_Needed: Transaction-by-transaction cash-flow ledger; beneficial ownership; bank records.

Exact_Claim: THCR used international hosts, comps, player tracking, and significant casino credit.
People_and_Entities: THCR casinos; international representatives; high-end patrons
Supporting_Evidence: SEC describes comps, player cards, international reps, credit percentages.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: These are standard casino practices and not inherently improper.
Recommended_Wording: The casino system created a documented interface for high-value relationships and requires patron-level testing.
Open_Question: Were exceptions made for connected or law-enforcement-linked patrons?
Additional_Record_Needed: Credit files; comp logs; travel records; CTR/SAR data; host correspondence.

Exact_Claim: Casino benefits may have been exchanged for property, financing, or introductions.
People_and_Entities: Trump entities; high-end patrons; brokers; lenders
Supporting_Evidence: Multiple benefit mechanisms existed and could be cross-matched.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: No specific quid pro quo yet documented.
Recommended_Wording: Cross-transaction reciprocity is a records question, not a current finding.
Open_Question: Do the same names appear in patron, property, mortgage, political, and contractor records?
Additional_Record_Needed: Unified entity-resolution database; patron records; deeds; mortgages; vendor ledgers.

Exact_Claim: Foreign rescue transactions could create future influence or access.
People_and_Entities: Asian, Saudi, Singaporean, Russian and other investors
Supporting_Evidence: Investors preserved assets and Trump roles; continued relationships possible.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Most transactions have conventional profit motives; no policy quid pro quo during period.
Recommended_Wording: Foreign dependence created potential leverage but must be tied to later requests or benefits.
Open_Question: Did investors later seek political, regulatory, or business favors?
Additional_Record_Needed: Communications; later deals; lobbying; governmental contacts; political donations.

Exact_Claim: The decade’s networks were centrally coordinated.
People_and_Entities: Banks; mob figures; Russian buyers; investors; Trump entities
Supporting_Evidence: Recurring nodes and compatible mechanisms exist.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Different actors, motives, jurisdictions and dates; no command structure documented.
Recommended_Wording: The evidence supports converging networks, not one proven centrally directed conspiracy.
Open_Question: Is there a common intermediary, account, agreement, or communications hub?
Additional_Record_Needed: Phone/email records; bank flows; corporate ownership; law-enforcement intelligence.

Exact_Claim: Chapter 4’s Bayrock/licensing model grew from 1990s structures.
People_and_Entities: Trump; Sapir; post-Soviet buyers; outside developers
Supporting_Evidence: 1990s show outside capital, brand contribution, presales, private credit and post-Soviet demand.
Counterevidence_or_Limit: Bayrock introduced distinct actors and alleged conduct.
Recommended_Wording: Bayrock expanded—not invented—the outside-capital and post-Soviet-intermediary model.
Open_Question: Which 1990s relationships directly introduced Chapter 4 partners?
Additional_Record_Needed: Contact calendars; tenancy; emails; broker and attorney files; deal proposals.
